commands.repair_reason: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can fix the duplicate trust-anchor validation/counting bug, while final merge still needs maintainer/security approval for the trust stack.; Needs attention: The diff introduces a signed marketplace trust boundary, and the current threshold validation can count duplicate key material as independent trust anchors.; Review finding: [P1] Reject duplicate trust anchors before thresholdin... commands.review_summary:
This PR adds signed marketplace feed config/envelope verification, wires signed hosted catalog refresh and snapshot trust persistence, and adds SQLite trust-state columns with tests.

PR surface: Source +474, Tests +611, Generated +10. Total +1095 across 17 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding: source inspection shows duplicate normalized public keys can pass config validation and the verifier counts distinct keyIds, not distinct key material. The PR body's live-output proof covers the happy signed-refresh path but not this threshold edge case.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can fix the duplicate trust-anchor validation/counting bug, while final merge still needs maintainer/security approval for the trust stack.

target.title: Verify signed marketplace feed refresh
target.body:
## Summary

- Wire signed marketplace feed profiles into hosted catalog refresh using the signed envelope verifier and configured threshold.
- Persist bounded signed-feed trust state with hosted snapshots, and re-verify signed snapshots with the current source profile before using them.
- Keep signed profiles fail-closed on invalid live bodies or unsigned old snapshots, without changing install eligibility or the existing marketplace refresh/entries payload shape.

## Validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog-envelope.test.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm db:kysely:check`
- `pnpm exec oxlint --deny-warnings src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog-envelope.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog-envelope.test.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog-snapshot-store.ts src/state/openclaw-state-db.ts src/state/openclaw-state-db.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog-envelope.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog-envelope.test.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog-snapshot-store.ts src/state/openclaw-state-db.ts src/state/openclaw-state-db.test.ts src/state/openclaw-state-schema.sql src/state/openclaw-state-schema.generated.ts src/state/openclaw-state-db.generated.d.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `codex review --base fork/feeds-trust-source-profiles`

## Real behavior proof

Source harness from this branch generated an Ed25519 key, signed a hosted catalog feed envelope, loaded it through a signed feed profile, verified persisted snapshot trust state, then attempted offline fallback from an unsigned old snapshot under the same signed profile.

```json
{
  "acceptedSource": "hosted",
  "acceptedEntries": [
    "@openclaw/signed-proof"
  ],
  "acceptedTrust": {
    "mode": "signed",
    "signedBy": "proof-root",
    "signatureCount": 1,
    "threshold": 1,
    "verifiedAt": "2026-07-01T00:00:01.000Z"
  },
  "snapshotTrust": {
    "mode": "signed",
    "signedBy": "proof-root",
    "signatureCount": 1,
    "threshold": 1,
    "verifiedAt": "2026-07-01T00:00:01.000Z"
  },
  "unsignedSnapshotRejected": true,
  "unsignedSnapshotError": "hosted catalog feed offline mode; snapshot fallback failed: hosted catalog signed envelope is malformed"
}
```

## Stack notes

This is OpenClaw trust series PR #4 / OpenClaw PR #3, stacked after:

- #98299 signed hosted feed envelope verifier primitives
- #98316 source-profile trust config

The PR currently targets `main` as a draft because the dependency branches are on the fork. Reviewers should focus on the top commit until the earlier trust PRs land.
target.author: giodl73-repo
